message:
fix(func): align builtin scalar functions with C SQLite NULL/edge-case semantics

Correct several functions that diverged from C SQLite behavior:

- char(): NULL arguments now produce U+0000 (NUL byte) instead of being
  silently skipped, matching sqlite3_value_int(NULL) returning 0.
- concat_ws(): NULL separator now returns NULL instead of empty string.
- hex(): hex(NULL) now returns '' (empty string) instead of NULL, matching
  C SQLite's blob(NULL)→empty behavior.
- iif(): Now strictly 3 arguments (no 2-arg form), matching C SQLite.
- zeroblob(): zeroblob(NULL) now returns x'' (empty blob) instead of NULL.
- printf/format: Add %x/%X (hex) and %o (octal) specifiers. Fix %s to
  render NULL as "(null)" and %q to render NULL as unquoted "NULL",
  matching C SQLite format semantics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
